Title: Identification of a Chlamydomonas plastidial 2-lysophosphatidic acid acyltransferase and its use to engineer microalgae with increased oil content

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 1-acyl-sn-glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase (LPAAT/ATS2 family); conserved acyltransferase motifs NHX4D, GVIFIDR, EGTR, IVPIVM; predicted transmembrane domains; plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage oil; relevant feedstock for bioenergy production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plastid and lipid droplets/oil bodies under nitrogen starvation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidial ('prokaryotic') glycerolipid pathway contributing to TAG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates the sn-2 position of 2-lysophosphatidic acid using acyl-CoA to form phosphatidic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; supported as an important control point sufficient to raise TAG content when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in plastids increases oil/TAG content by >20% under nitrogen-deficient conditions and preferentially increases C16-containing TAG species (TAG50, TAG52); also elevates polar membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Moderate in mid-log phase cells; induced about threefold after 12 h nitrogen starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Moderate in mid-log phase cells; induced about threefold after 12 h in TAP-N medium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli plsC mutant complementation; in vitro enzyme assay with membrane fractions; transient plastid-targeting assay in tobacco; fluorescent protein localization in Chlamydomonas; plastid overexpression; LC-MS/MS lipid profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cre09.g398289 encodes an authentic plastid-targeted LPAAT in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii. 2. CrLPAAT1 catalyzes PA formation from LPA and shows strong preference for 16:0-CoA over 18:1-CoA. 3. CrLPAAT1 transcript is induced by nitrogen starvation. 4. Plastid overexpression increases TAG/oil by about 20% under nitrogen starvation, especially C16-containing TAG species, and also raises membrane lipid levels.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: HSP70A-RBCS2 chimeric promoter (constitutive/high-expression nuclear expression for localization); plastid expression from pLM21 plastid transgene cassette, promoter details NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS lipidomics; qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to increase algal oil production, especially by plastid genome transformation; may be stacked with other TAG-pathway genes to enhance flux through the plastidial/prokaryotic branch; best deployed under nitrogen-limitation; monitor accompanying increases in membrane lipids and possible carbon partition effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased polar membrane lipids alongside TAG indicate broader lipid remodeling; effects on growth/yield not clearly established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full-length protein detected as precursor and mature processed forms in plastid overexpressors, consistent with transit peptide cleavage; source gene and applied species are the same in main functional tests, but transit peptide was also validated in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
